web.markdown:
![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/03/How-To-Write-A-Sales-Letter-That-Will-Make-You-Rich-450x247.png)

# Sales Letter & Killer Bullets from Scott Haines

[Copywriting](https://swiped.co/niche/copywriting)[Sales Letter](https://swiped.co/types/sales-letter)[Scott Haines](https://swiped.co/person/scott-haines)

[Control](https://swiped.co/file/sales-letter-from-scott-haines/#)

Ran for two years before Scott stopped selling the product.

swiped byMike Schauerfrom [killercopysecrets.com/1996seminar.html](http://www.killercopysecrets.com/1996seminar.html) ran2003-2005

Was tipped off to this sales letter from Ben Settle in his how to build a swipe file report that you can download here: [http://www.wsi-ic.com/fmuscat/FileContent/BNI%20Instant-Swipe-File.pdf](http://www.wsi-ic.com/fmuscat/FileContent/BNI%20Instant-Swipe-File.pdf)
Scott...View More attended Gary Halbert's 1996 seminar "How To Write A Sales Letter That Will Make You Rich" & is selling the transcript in this letter. Ben had mentioned that Scott is a master at writing bullets & I couldn't agree more. More than half of this letter is composed of bullets & they really kick ass.

## ![Sales Letter & Killer Bullets from Scott Haines](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/03/How-To-Write-A-Sales-Letter-That-Will-Make-You-Rich.png?cachefix)

Sales Letter & Killer Bullets from Scott Haines

Swiped in February 2014

Ran 2003-2005

[Get Swipe Updates](https://swiped.co/file/sales-letter-from-scott-haines/#signup)

![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/plugins/wti-like-post-pro/images/pixel.gif)408

#### Analyst Spotlight

![Mike Schauer](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2018/01/Mike-Schauer_avatar-125x125.jpg)

Mike Schaueris the founder of Swiped.co and the main analyst in the swipes section. After intently studying & building conversion-focused websites for 6+ years, he started Swiped to help others master marketing & copywriting through the analysis of great examples!

![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/03/How-To-Write-A-Sales-Letter-That-Will-Make-You-Rich-450x247.png)

# Sales Letter & Killer Bullets from Scott Haines

[Copywriting](https://swiped.co/niche/copywriting)[Sales Letter](https://swiped.co/types/sales-letter)[Scott Haines](https://swiped.co/person/scott-haines)

[Control](https://swiped.co/file/sales-letter-from-scott-haines/1000/#)

Ran for two years before Scott stopped selling the product.

swiped byMike Schauerfrom [killercopysecrets.com/1996seminar.html](http://www.killercopysecrets.com/1996seminar.html) ran2003-2005

Was tipped off to this sales letter from Ben Settle in his how to build a swipe file report that you can download here: [http://www.wsi-ic.com/fmuscat/FileContent/BNI%20Instant-Swipe-File.pdf](http://www.wsi-ic.com/fmuscat/FileContent/BNI%20Instant-Swipe-File.pdf)
Scott...View More attended Gary Halbert's 1996 seminar "How To Write A Sales Letter That Will Make You Rich" & is selling the transcript in this letter. Ben had mentioned that Scott is a master at writing bullets & I couldn't agree more. More than half of this letter is composed of bullets & they really kick ass.

[All Tags](https://swiped.co/tags)

# Modern

Often pertaining to design that is relevant and up to date, modern design exemplifies the latest design trends, which today include usage of flat colors, more white space, premium fonts & often, more concise messaging. Being modern does not always equal better. In fact, being too modern is often counter-productive since you’re putting the look & feel on a pedestal when your focus should be on the strategy & copy.

# Long Copy

A detailed and lengthy approach to describing the product or service, its uses, benefits, features and the like versus those of competitive products. As a general rule, expensive or complex products or unique services require longer copy — since it is sometimes necessary to “educate” the prospect.

web.markdown:
![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/halbert-11amazingsecrets-recesssion-349x247.png)

# Lead Gen Ad by Gary Halbert

[Info Marketing](https://swiped.co/niche/info-marketing)[Lead Generation](https://swiped.co/types/lead-generation)[Print Ad](https://swiped.co/types/print-ad)[Gary Halbert](https://swiped.co/person/gary-halbert)

[Classic](https://swiped.co/classics)

swiped byMike Schauerran1990-1991[1](https://swiped.co/file/lead-gen-ad-by-gary-halbert/#comments)

These two ads were run multiple times by Gary Halbert in 1990-1991 to ultimately sell a $295 package of his newsletters & some videos. The sales letter for...View More that package is here: [http://swiped.co/file/famous-dollar-letter-by-gary-halbert/](http://swiped.co/file/famous-dollar-letter-by-gary-halbert/) If you replied to the ad & called the phone number, Gary likely got your name & address to send his "report" which was really a sales letter pitching the $295 package. The ad is composed of bullets Gary selected from his longer ads such as his Desperate Nerd ad here: [http://swiped.co/file/amazing-money-making-secrets-ad-by-gary-halbert](http://swiped.co/file/amazing-money-making-secrets-ad-by-gary-halbert) It's a great example of taking existing copy that works for you & re-purposing it for other ads & promotions. In this case, it's clear that Gary was testing out what amount of bullets & headline converted best.



## ![Lead Gen Ad by Gary Halbert](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/halbert-11amazingsecrets-recesssion.png)

## ![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/garyhalbert-leadgen-wallstreet.png)

Lead Gen Ad by Gary Halbert

Swiped in February 2014

Ran 1990-1991

[Get Swipe Updates](https://swiped.co/file/lead-gen-ad-by-gary-halbert/#signup)

![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/plugins/wti-like-post-pro/images/pixel.gif)61

#### Analyst Spotlight

![Mike Schauer](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2018/01/Mike-Schauer_avatar-125x125.jpg)

Mike Schaueris the founder of Swiped.co and the main analyst in the swipes section. After intently studying & building conversion-focused websites for 6+ years, he started Swiped to help others master marketing & copywriting through the analysis of great examples!
